channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump refocuses his message on 'winning' as broad support for the war in Iran wavers
item.description: As the U.S. war against Iran enters its third week, the Trump administration is attempting to refocus its messaging as broader support for the war falters.
item.content:encoded: As the U.S. war against Iran enters its third week, the Trump administration is attempting to refocus its messaging as broader support for the war falters.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Washington Post's David Ignatius says war on Iran won't change the country's regime
item.description: Michel Martin speaks to Washington Post columnist David Ignatius, who writes that the U.S. war on Iran, despite any tactical success, leaves the regime in place for the foreseeable future.
item.content:encoded: Michel Martin speaks to Washington Post columnist David Ignatius, who writes that the U.S. war on Iran, despite any tactical success, leaves the regime in place for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Oil and gas prices are soaring. Some countries are ready with solar panels and EVs
item.description: As an energy crisis grows, some countries are more prepared because of renewable energy and electric vehicles. Pakistan reduced its reliance on imported natural gas because of the growth of solar.
item.content:encoded:
As an energy crisis grows, some countries are more prepared because of renewable energy and electric vehicles. Pakistan reduced its reliance on imported natural gas because of the growth of solar.

(Image credit: Asif Hassan)
item.dc:creator: Julia Simon

item.title: Influencers push 'parasite cleanses' but doctors say to steer clear
item.description: Some people online believe many of us have dangerous parasites in our gut and need to flush them out with herbal supplements. Here's what doctors say about the trend.
item.content:encoded: Some people online believe many of us have dangerous parasites in our gut and need to flush them out with herbal supplements. Here's what doctors say about the trend.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The war with Iran enters its third week, as Trump and his top aides refocus their messaging on "winning" to regain faltering support, in the Senate, Republican lawmakers take up the SAVE Act this week.
item.content:encoded: The war with Iran enters its third week, as Trump and his top aides refocus their messaging on "winning" to regain faltering support, in the Senate, Republican lawmakers take up the SAVE Act this week.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Georgetown Law professor on the SAVE Act and Trump's desire to control elections
item.description: What is the SAVE America Act and how does it fit into President Trump's larger ambition to control elections? NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Georgetown Law professor Steve Vladeck.
item.content:encoded: What is the SAVE America Act and how does it fit into President Trump's larger ambition to control elections? NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Georgetown Law professor Steve Vladeck.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: FCC chair threatens broadcasters' licenses over negative coverage of the war in Iran
item.description: President Trump, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and FCC Chair Brendan Carr have assailed the media over coverage of the war in Iran. Carr now threatens broadcast license owners with repercussions.
item.content:encoded: President Trump, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and FCC Chair Brendan Carr have assailed the media over coverage of the war in Iran. Carr now threatens broadcast license owners with repercussions.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: 'One Battle After Another,' 'Sinners' win big on Oscars night. A roundup of the night
item.description: "One Battle After Another' took home best picture at the Oscars while Michael B. Jordan won best actor for his role as twins Smoke and Stack in "Sinners." A look at the winners, surprises and snubs.
item.content:encoded: "One Battle After Another' took home best picture at the Oscars while Michael B. Jordan won best actor for his role as twins Smoke and Stack in "Sinners." A look at the winners, surprises and snubs.
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Hungary's longtime prime minister rallies to keep seat as rival calls for a new era
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban slams Europe in an election speech on his country's Revolution Day.
item.content:encoded: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban slams Europe in an election speech on his country's Revolution Day.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Iran blocks vital oil route as Israel expands its ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.
item.content:encoded: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin
